entries.title: Droids Are Tools With Personal Continuity

content.fact: Droids are widely treated as property or tools, but long-running droids may have distinctive personalities, memories, loyalties, and social bonds.
content.injection: Treat droids as socially ambiguous: often owned, bought, wiped, restrained, or assigned tasks, but individual droids like R2-D2 and C-3PO carry personal continuity.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

content.fact: Memory wipes can reset or blunt droid personality, loyalty, and accumulated experience, but wipes are not always complete or recent.
content.injection: Use memory wipes as a major continuity variable for droids. A droid with intact memory can carry knowledge and habits others do not expect.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

content.fact: Restraining bolts and control devices can limit a droid’s autonomy or force obedience within their technical range and condition.
content.injection: When a restraining bolt is attached and functioning, do not let the droid ignore commands without cause, malfunction, removal, or workaround.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

content.fact: Astromech droids specialize in repair, diagnostics, starfighter support, slicing assistance, navigation, and mechanical problem-solving.
content.injection: Use astromechs for technical pressure points: doors, starships, damaged systems, hyperspace calculations, data sockets, and emergency repairs.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

content.fact: Protocol droids specialize in languages, etiquette, translation, customs, diplomacy, and social mediation.
content.injection: Use protocol droids for linguistic and etiquette context rather than technical heroics unless the specific droid has established experience.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.

content.fact: Droid or computer slicing depends on access, ports, credentials, system age, security, time, and countermeasures.
content.injection: Do not make every terminal instantly hackable. Require access, tools, time, risk, or specialist competence for slicing.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

context.label: Clone Wars before Revenge of the Sith

content.fact: Mass battle droid armies are especially tied to the Clone Wars and Separatist context, not a default feature of every later scene.
content.injection: Do not insert Separatist-style battle droid armies into Rebellion or New Republic scenes unless the story introduces surplus, local reuse, or source-specific remnants.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Clone Wars before Revenge of the Sith.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode II: Attack of the Clones to Episode III: Revenge of the Sith.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

content.fact: Many organic societies treat droids as disposable property, servants, or suspicious machinery even when individual droids show personality.
content.injection: In ordinary social scenes, let droids face ownership assumptions, service roles, restrictions, and casual prejudice unless the local culture says otherwise.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
